title: SNAP 75% Mechanical Keyboard Kit (White)
description: A stylish, 75% split The SNAP is a 75% split mechanical keyboard kit with support for 2 rotary encoders, 2 OLED displays, speaker, and RGB underglow. We took all the best parts of the NIBBLE and added some of the most popular requests: true split shift/space/backspace. The SNAP is configurable, programmable, and easily assembled. 100% through hole design The SNAP uses a Pro Micro MCU instead of a discrete micro-controller chip. It offers the fun and aesthetic of a kit without having to worry about soldering the difficult bits like USB connectors. All required parts diodes, caps, and ICs) are through-hole, meaning they're easy and forgiving to solder. underglow LEDs are surface-mount Multiple Layout Options Use the SNAP in split mode, or snap it whoa, get it?) together for a more traditional layout. Strong internal magnets hold the two sides together and magical spring-loaded pogo pin contacts mean there's no need for a TRRS cable when the two sides are attached. The SNAP supports many configurations and layouts: Split space/backspace, split shift, ISO enter, and more. Since it runs QMK firmware and uses VIA, it's easy to change keymaps, program macros, and customize functionality. RGB underglow ready 10x optional bottom-mounted SMT LEDs shine nicely through the acrylic support material. Included Top & bottom PCB Acrylic supports M2 standoffs & screws Diodes, ICs, decoupling caps, reset switch RGB underglow, speaker, magnets, and pogo pins Rotary encoder & color-matched knob TRRS cable and mini bumpons Not included 2x Bit-Cs or Pro Micro Compatible MCU MX-compatible switches & keycaps PCB-mount stabilizers NOTE: This is a kit which requires assembly and soldering! Bit-Cs, switches, keycaps, and other accessories are sold separately
features: The SNAP is a high-quality split 75% keyboard kit with tons of room for customization
Use the SNAP together, or separate! It snaps together for a traditional 75% layout or pulls apart for use as a split board
Built around a Pro Micro, rather than a discrete microcontroller. This saves assembly time, and also reduces the build difficulty
All required parts (diodes, caps, and ICs) are through-hole, meaning they're easy and forgiving to solder

RGB underglow ready: 10x optional bottom-mounted SMT LEDs shine nicely through the acrylic support material. RGB underglow is supported natively in QMK, allowing for easy programming and configuration

reviews.title: Not for the faint of heart
reviews.text:
This kit does take a lot of work so I would not recommend it as a first build, or for someone looking for a quick build.

It is also a fairly unique one of a kind kit. I don't know of other split 75% keyboards with pro micro support that also have great documentation, support and community. If you are willing to put in the time you will be rewarded with a unique keyboard, and the kit really is fun to put together for experienced builders.

I would knock down half star as I encountered some issues with the fitting of the acrylic plate, specifically in these areas:
1. acrylic plate fitting at the keyboard split edge, had to use an exacto knife
2. acrylic plate fitting with bottom screws of the PCB mount stabilizers, had to make the holes wider
3. snap magnets did not fit, gave up trying to fit these
4. components pin through the solder joints interfered with acrylic, this is expected and is easily fixable by trimming them down.

Apart from that everything worked as expected. Firmware worked on first try and I was easily able to get it working with VIA.

title: Qupei Metal Keycaps OEM(12 Keys) FPS/MOBA for 60%/87/104/108 fot MX Switches Mechanical Keyboard, Ultra-Thin FPS & MOBA Gaming Keyboard Caps for Cherry Mx Switch (Silver)
features: This metal keycaps are made of 100% Stainless Steel. The thickness is around 1.2mm. The legend is laser shot and shine through. Super wear-resistant, no fading, strong corrosion resistance, clear characters, dedicate touch with drawing process, not deformed. Make your common keyboards special and textured.

The keycaps are OEM Profile, R1~R4 layout have different height and angle slope, ergonomic key shape designed to provide a comfortable typing experience while using your computer keyboard. This keycaps belong to ANSI/ISO layout, so it's fit for most 60%/87/104/108 MX Switches Mechanical Keyboard.

Personalize your keyboard. Visually striking colors and smooth textured design keys help you immediately spot the specific function keys. Hollow out lettering keys for crystal clear supports backlight which was made using Laser Carving technique.

No worries about missing or worn lettering anymore. Designed for longevity with greater durability and responsiveness. This link only sells OEM profile Keycaps, no keyboard included. There is a puller presented in package, you can easily pull out and replace it.
The Keycaps has two-year quality warranty, if there is any quality problem, you could contact us in any time, and we will help you solve the problem in 12 hours.

reviews.title: Good with some room for improvement
reviews.text:
So I got these keycaps today and installed. They installed just fine on Cherry style switches. They have a satisfying *thock* sound to them when depressing. I mostly got them for arrow and WASD. The main thing I'm noticing is that the top edges of the keycap, while aren't sharp in the sense that you may cut yourself, could really use just a TOUCH of softening.

Also, the letters and arrows are actual cutouts. it's possible for skin and debris to pass through them, so handle with care? I haven't had them long enough to know if it makes more of a mess than standard pudding keycaps. Also don't know if the heavier metal keycaps have any effect on longevity. Definitely need less force to depress the keys.

To get 5 stars, I think just softening the edges of the tops of the keycaps and maybe finding a way to have epoxy resin fill to seal the holes without affecting weigh or backlight visibility would be the only things I ask for. Keep the dead skin cells and snack food dust from slipping through the top of the keycap.

reviews.title: Good Quality Keycaps
reviews.text: I kept breaking the plastic keycaps on my Corsair K95 RGB keyboard while playing Doom Eternal. These keycaps are made of aluminum and are holding much better. They fit a little tighter than the plastic keycaps, but they do fit.

reviews.title: Can be used stock!
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:528307786b36a4c2c9833ddd389101cb]] They come with a small amount of lube pre applied. Usually, prelubed switches are inconsistent in my experience but these ones are really good. I decided to not lube them at all. EDIT: got a few more batches, they're not as consistent. But seller was nice enough to get them replaced.

The stems are long pole. As a result, it has a nice bottom out sound.

It was noticeably heavier coming from a 50g switch to these that are 63g, but overtime I got adjusted and had no problems typing with them.

Overall a great buy, though I wish it was priced more competitively.

Using on Drop Preonic with MT3 Camillo, plateless, tape mod

reviews.title: Nice linears
reviews.text:
Starting off not sure who's at fault for this as it's only fulfilled by Amazon but one of my bags was missing a few switches (one bag was open and all the switches were rattling around inside the amazon pouch it arrived in) I counted 5 missing and there wasn't a silica gel packet either, the other bags were fine.
complaint aside they feel really nice and the factory lube seems to be well done as wish they had a "3 pin" option and different switch amounts 35 2 is too few and 3 is too many (unless some get lost in transit like mine lol) 90 would be a good option for the TKL/75% gang

reviews.title: Will be Using Right Away
reviews.text: In building & repairing computers these key switches are used very often. Mainly for high-end (over $100) keyboards that the serious gamers use. What they do is tough on the keyboards and often switches break or become less responsive which may lead to a gamer losing a match. These snap right in to the sockets and can instantly rebuild the whole keyboard like new. I realize they would not be used by your everyday person or casual gamer but when it gets serious they are  the switches to be used.

reviews.title: Nice Linear
reviews.text: These are nice and smooth and sound wonderful.  I'm not a linear fan usually, but I do like them in my modifier keys (shift, backspace, enter, and space) because I don't like the return sound a tactile makes while in a larger key.  These are perfect for that as they don't have a high pitch and there's no spring ping to even think of!  I would highly recommend them to anyone looking for a lighter-weight linear switch with some style.

reviews.title: Great Stock!
reviews.text: The switches sounds great and feels good to use. The lube in this is not bad at all and you don't hear any ping or scratchiness. The bottom out is much more than I am used to, but I do enjoy it otherwise.

title: Kraken Coiled Keyboard Cable - Artisan Coiled USB C Cable with Aviator Connector for Mechanical Gaming Keyboards (Pure White)
features: Premium Artisan Designs in colors to fit every gaming setup!

Double Sleeved for enhanced looks and durability!

reviews.title: Excellent quality cable and connectors, but color is more purple than blue
reviews.text:
Update: Oddly enough, this did not work with a Drop Alt keyboard. It worked with a few other keyboards just fine though. Just a word of warning for anyone pairing this cable with a Drop Alt (or maybe any other Drop keyboard).

This is the third coiled cable I have ordered, and it's definitely the best quality overall. The cable itself is thick and good quality. The coil is a bit firm, but nothing too unusual. The intermediate connector is all metal and heavy. Both sides of the intermediate connector can be unthreaded: The side going to the USB-C connector, and the side going to the USB connector.

One interesting thing I noticed was that the end connectors have a color matched almost shrink wrap thickness strain protection around them. The USB side is the most noticeable, and the USB-C side is a bit thicker. As long as there is enough strain protection, I actually prefer the less bulky end connections.

The only nit is the color. Even though it looked dark purplish in the product listing, I was hoping that it would be more blue in real life. It's not, it's an attractive dark purple. I would assume the color represented in the product listing is accurate, in case you're wondering if there is any color variation from the listing photos to real life.

title: ASUS ROG Falchion Wireless 65% Mechanical Gaming Keyboard | 68 Keys, Aura Sync RGB, Extended Battery Life, Interactive Touch Panel, PBT Keycaps, Cherry MX Red Switches, Keyboard Cover Case
description: COMPACT, WITHOUT COMPROMISE ROG Falchion is a 65% form-factor wireless mechanical keyboard designed to change the way you play. It has an interactive touch panel and innovative two-way cover case, and it’s the first ROG keyboard with wireless Aura Sync RGB lighting. The keys feature durable ROG polybutylene terephthalate (PBT) doubleshot keycaps and German-made Cherry MX RGB mechanical switches for precise, tactile keystrokes. ROG Falchion utilizes a gaming-grade 2.4 GHz RF connection for 1 ms report rate and offers up to 450 hours of battery life on a single charge.
features: Compact 65% layout masterfully incorporates arrow and navigation keys, in a 60% keyboard frame
Interactive touch panel makes it easy to adjust and customize controls
Dual-mode connectivity via wired USB and 1 ms at 2.4 GHz RF, and up to 450 hours of battery life* for stable, long-lasting performance (*with RGB lighting off)
Durable and long-lasting ROG PBT doubleshot keycaps
Innovative keyboard cover case provides on-the-go protection

reviews.title: More updates than microsoft!
reviews.text:
First of all, its a DUMB keyboard, meaning, you cant save the profile TO THE KEYBOARD... For this price, you think they could have bout a 64k ram chip in it...

So this means, that every time I hit my KVM, it goes to default, then I have to open the software to get my color profile back and EVERY SINGLE TIME I OPEN IT... It has to update the software... EVERY FLIPPIN DAY!

I hate this keyboard! 3 stars are too many!

Seriously, ASUS, I love your gaming laptop, but your software is garbage... Hire better programmers or even better, let me move the color profile to the KEYBOARD, so I never have to load your crappy software ever again!

Then after this loading screen loads for 5 minutes, I end up having to restart my computer to get it work again...

Seriously, this keyboard needs more updates than M$ and you have to restart your machine to use it after... if you want a keyboard that sux less, see 2nd image...

Final Update: I just updated the software and now it wont even connect to the keyboard anymore... Im DONE! Just ordered a DROP ALT, it has build in memory, so I can program the color profile and its WRITTEN TO THE KEYBOARD, no more daily updates, and constant frustration to get the stupid software to work, just so I can keep the color profile I want to be ALL THE TIME...

Good hardware, keyboard is solid, software ruined the product!

reviews.title: The RGB lights will sleep and won't wake up... annoying
reviews.text:
I bought this keyboard both for work and play. I use it for work during the day, and play games during the night. It's been a couple of months since I got this keyboard, and I love it. I love the form factor, I love the feel typing with it. Excellent for gaming. But with all that said, there is one annoying thing that warrants it to get 3 stars... the RGB lighting.

I got this plugged in to my 43 inch Aorus gaming monitor's KVM. When I work, this keyboard controls my work laptop, and when I game it controls my gaming PC via KVM. The RGB works fine on both computers, but when the computers goes to sleep and/or the screen turns off, the RGB also turns off. It's fine when it turns on when I wake up the computers, but majority of the time the RGB lights won't come back on from sleep, and that is annoying. When I manually lock the desktop, the RGB lights on the keyboard turns off, but when I unlock the desktop, the RGB lights won't turn on also. I thought something may be wrong with the KVM, but I tested the KVM using my Keychron keyboards, and they work fine, which means nothing is wrong with the KVM of the monitor. In order for the lights to work again, I have to unplug and re-plug the USB cable. It does the same thing even when in wireless mode.

I honestly like this keyboard when the RGB lights are working, and right now just keep on checking if there are new firmware for this keyboard everyday hoping that the annoying sleep bug will be fixed. So, 3 stars for now.

reviews.title: Nice keyboard bad software
reviews.text: The armory crate software is the worst I’ve used in my life. The actual keyboard is fine.

reviews.title: best keyboard I’ve ever had
reviews.text: I love this keyboard, had no idea that the type of plastic material the keys are made of makes a huge difference in how it feels and how enjoyable it is to use. The mechanical switches are great too but I sill really like this keyboard for the texture of the keys. I have nothing negative to say hope to have this keyboard for the next 20 years.

reviews.title: x2 Review Update - Great Keyboard
reviews.text:
Works great. Anyone looking at this keyboard already knows what it is and what it can do. I've heard it is a little more expensive than other brands that are doing the same thing. I don't know or care. I wanted this one, this brand. It's been flawless since purchase. - older review

8/26/22 - Update
Original keyboard is still working flawless since purchase. This keyboard has been working so well that I actually ended up buying a second one! I jump between multi-devices daily. Running the USB-C (charging cable) to one device and placing the Bluetooth dongle in another allows you to easily switch between them. You could very well run a USB-C cable to all your devices using color coded cables with some cable management have a USB-C (fountain like) connected to your desk and rotate through the cables to match the desired device.

This keyboard runs flawless on every device I have associated it with. Zero issues in regard to connectivity: brands, operating systems, etc.

Have to keep in mind that the heart of this keyboard is gaming. Very fast and instant with zero noticeable latency & lag. Wired - Bluetooth you will not be able to tell the difference. Large hands might get a little cramped. I've learned to work through it. The pros outweigh the cons. The only cons I can think that others would consider as such, would be the size and price. But you can't really fault it for that and here is why. 1) It's a 60-65% keyboard and you knew this prior of purchase. You can't fault it for what it is and its targeted users. i.e. "Well, it's not as comfortable on the arthritis as a full-size split keyboard" - duh... 2) You get what you pay for. There are more expensive kb's as well as cheaper. Rog Falchion sits nestled in the lower mid-range tier on pricing. Blue Clicky are on sale for $99.99 and the Red Linear are $129.99. That is a very fair price considering what is packaged.

Rog software is not the best but isn't the worst either. I have had a lot more issues with my Logitech software in comparison to what has basically been a stress-free experience with Rog. The software is not technically required to use the kb. If you want to make customizations such as changing the kb color, etc. You will have to download it. There are stock functions (modes) built-in that may suffice enough for some users or turn the RGB completely off to utilize maximum battery life. ** Side note, I'm rarely ever in a need to charge it. I leave RGB on at all times. I've left it on overnight, etc. The physical need to charge in order for me to use it is so far and few in-between that it feels like a chargeless keyboard. If you use a USB-C to connect to another device the Keyboard will charge while doing so.

Mine has been pretty damn resilient too. I have unintentionally knocked/thrown this keyboard via airborne a few times from my working area onto vinyl flooring. Besides a few small scratches on the case, little harm was done each time.

So, what do we have here?

We have a mechanical keyboard with MX Cherries. One has Red Linear switches and the other Blue Clicky.

I prefer the Blue Clicky!!! This keyboard is oh, so satisfying.

Yes, they are loud - oh no! However, no one cares. You don't purchase mechanical keyboards while pretending to be an office ninja. That is why I prefer the Blue Clicky over the Red linear.

The Red linear knows it's loud but tries to be quiet while at a higher cost. It delivers what feels like a finger-press to the depths of the Mariana Trench with an awkward/muffled low-toned like sound to match the embarrassment it is hiding from.

The Blue Clicky fully embraces its true self while doing so at a lower cost. It delivers a more satisfying user experience with touch and sound. Each key press has an instant-mechanical-click. Letting you know, "Hey I am here!" Delivering the sound and feeling of reaching the finish line with each finger press.

Overall - It is a wonderful keyboard. The Falchion will never make you feel like you wish it had more abilities. It will only make you feel like you want more of its capabilities. - Hints me buying two

Regardless of your switch preference you can't go wrong with this purchase.

title: Fogruaden Pudding Keycaps 60 Percent, 118 Dye-Sublimation Keycaps Set, ASA Profile Custom Keycaps for Cherry Gateron MX Switches Mechanical Keyboard(Journey)
features: [Pudding Keycaps] Pudding keycaps are specifically designed to amplify the RGB backlighting of keyboards.The lower half of these keycaps are designed to be completely translucent, enabling the backlight of a keyboard to shine through easily, maximize the shine-through of your keyboard’s RGB backlighting.
[Compatibility] This keycaps set has 118 keys, suitable for most 61/68/87/104 ANSI US layouts cherry mx switches keyboards; ideal for custom mechanical keyboards choose

reviews.title: In Love!
reviews.text: I purchased the "Life is a Journey" ASA profile pudding keycaps because of the awesome theme and color scheme. They are gorgeous, sophisticated, and they FEEL better than any other pudding keycaps I've ever had. No wobble, no cheap plastic sound. They add to the sound of the board and typing experience. Superior to HyperX. I have them paired with my Kailh Box Whites and I couldn't be happier.

title: DAIDAI AJAZZ Diced Fruit Blueberry Keyboard Switch | 5 Pin 35gf Light Force Pre Lubed Linear Switch | Durable 80M MX Switch for Mechanical Keyboard (36PCS | Blueberry Switch)
features: ♫♫♫AJAZZ Diced Fruit blueberry switch for mechanical keyboard top cap and button part of the housing are made of transparent light blue and dark blue, blue-gray POM shaft core adds dust-proof wall, which increases the stability when pressing, and provides you with a smoother typing experience.

♫♫♫5 pin keyboard blueberry switches is the latest light pressure linear switch are made with operating force of 35gf and total travel of 3.8mm, 15.5mm double spring is more smooth when typing, it is perfectly compatible with 5pin hotswap mechanical keyboard.

♫♫♫Mechanical keyboard switch adopts MX structure design, which is widely compatible with most keycap sets with (X) crossbar, LED card slot and transparent PC material, which enhances the light transmission of keyboard LED lights, provide more choices and fun for fans who like personalized keyboard. (Note: Switch opener not included)

♫♫♫Prelubed linear switches in order to improve the smoothness and durability, it has been lubricated before leaving the factor, and the rebound is faster and smoother when the switch is pressed. The experimental test has exceeded 80 million keystrokes.

♫♫♫Friendly packaging design, open the packaging with a single switch, in order to experience the click feel to DIY enthusiasts, if you don't like it, you can return it, the remaining 35PCS switches are stored in transparent round cans, a bit like canned fruit, it is more convenient to store and carry it, perfect for gifting to yourself, friends or family.

♫♫♫AJAZZ fruit series custom switches in order to satisfy you who like DIY, we introduce different tactile or linear switches, whether beginners, professional gamers, typists and programmers can choose the most suitable AJAZZ fruit series switches. Create a unique typing experience for you.

reviews.title: Light, snappy, like a punchier Gat brown
reviews.text:
I previously got a tactile switch sampler set and honestly loved the AJAZZ Banana more than U4 Boba and its other iterations. I liked these more than Glorious Panda and Holy Panda. I type a lot as a student, so I didn't want anything too heavy--it gets tiring. I've been using AJAZZ Banana for two days so far now and I LOVE it!

I was using lubed Gateron browns before. I wanted something punchier but not too heavy. Banana is the perfect answer to that. Actuation force is about the same to Gat browns. I have a north-facing RGB board and the lights come through Banana switches beautifully.

In answer to the other reviewer, I believe that the Banana switches ARE lubed (with what, I don't know). I have a stock/un-lubed AJAZZ Banana switch from my switch sampler pack, these pre-lubed ones feel slightly lighter in comparison. Out of the box, these feel great without additional lubing. Results will vary depending on your mech keeb setup.

Note that the switch pack comes with 46 switches, so it won't be enough to do a full board. I have a 60% board and had enough to do the alphanumeric keys and other frequently pressed keys like Shift, Enter, Ctrl, Backspace, Alt, Tab, and punctuation keys. I left my Gat browns on the less frequent keys. I have two "extra" Banana switches as backups.

Highly recommend. If you want tactile but not heavy, this is a very affordable option.

reviews.title: Bananas are excellent tactile switch alternatives to browns and blues.
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:dff950435419a7006b0295e50b846c44]] This review is for the Ajazz Diced Fruit Banana Switch.
I wanted to try these switches compared to a brown switch because of its similar characteristics.
These Banana switches are definitely tactile and smooth. They do have a tactile click sound and require a bit more force to actuate (not really noticeable for me because I'm heavy handed and I bottom out the key when I type). I'm not going to mention specs such as actuation force and distance because you can find that info in the product description or manufacturer's site.
I will talk about the "feeling" of these switches though because in everyday use, gaming and office work, I'm not thinking about the actuation force/distance, etc. For me it is how it feels and sounds.

So first off, these are excellent switches, no doubt. It is then just a matter of personal preference whether or not you like the characteristics of the Banana switch. For me, it is a bit too noisy. The tactile bump feels good but after using the Banana's for a while, I would hate typing while on mic because the mic picks up the keystrokes loud and clear and can be annoying for others to hear. I don't have blue switches to compare these Bananas to but I would imagine the blue switches would be worse in sound. I do have red switches to compare and the reds are noticeably quieter, I think mainly due to being linear (no tactile bump). For me and personal preference and usage, I would stick with the reds.

If you like tactile switches and are currently using a different tactile switch, I'd urge you give these Banana switches a try. See my video review where I installed these switches into a keyboard and compare to red switches, so you can get an idea of the sound when the switches are installed into a keyboard frame with keycaps on. In the video I have mistakenly called the red switches Gaterons, but I'm pretty sure they are Kailh reds with box stem.

reviews.title: Nice looking strong tactile
reviews.text:
I love tactile switches, so when this was offered for review I figured I'd give it a shot as I'd heard good things about Ajazz switches. It arrived and I swapped out the switches in my NK65 (DROP Holy Pandas) and threw these into the alphas.

In terms of feel they're a pretty strong tactile. I felt it important to mention that I was using HP's above because these feel very similar to those, just with a lighter spring. The bump is close to identical which is nice, and I prefer the lighter spring as I've been mainlining my Niz lately with 35g switches, so light is my thing right now. In terms of sound, the housing does make these quite a bit higher pitch than HP's, and I'd honestly put it in line with some Akko tactiles. Not too high to not enjoy, but higher than I'm initially used to.

Because these come pre-lubed they can be used stock, which is nice because while once I start I find lubing switches to be relaxing, but it's an activity I dread until I'm actually going. Just something about it seems beyond tedious, and to be fair, it is. Thankfully the lube job on these is pretty good and there's no ping to be heard or scratchiness to be felt. I did lube one just to see the difference that one done by hand can make, and the job on these is good enough that it makes no difference. The switch is fine as-is.

Other than being plate mount only I can't find a problem with these. The spring weight is nice and they sound and feel great to type on. I'm thinking about grabbing another set to build out a board with them in the future.

reviews.title: Kiwi switches
reviews.text:
This is a review for the Kiwi Switch from DAIDAI AJAZZ. The Kiwi switch is tactile, 50gf, and housed in glittery translucent green. If you have clear keycaps, you can see the yellow stem and a little of the upper housing.

For the Kiwi switches, you get 46 switches. For most keyboards, 46 switches is not enough and you will need 2-3 cans.

The switches come in a clear plastic can, with a screw lid and a pull tab. The packaging is cute! More importantly, the can takes up less space than the usual plastic tray, and it’s reusable.

I use Akko Vintage Whites (linear), but I wanted to try tactile switches for the thumb clusters on my ergo keyboard, so I had feedback when I pressed those keys. The tactile bump is smooth and pronounced, I definitely know I pressed that key.

I didn’t notice any difference between these pre-lubed Kiwi switches and non-lubed switches.

reviews.title: Pretty good tactile switches
reviews.text: I've tried linears and even clicky switches, but I really prefer tacticles. These are right in the sweet spot for me in terms of spring weight and tactile bump. Nice colors, if that matters to you. I got these to try out before buying a full set, and I like them quite a bit.

It is a good keycap for DIY keycap literacy and basic popularization.134 keycaps can be used for most ANSI US layout mechanical keyboards, and any key switch with a cross-shaped switchs.Great for DIY keyboard use

reviews.title: Spacebars do not fit well
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:14b0c07be1bf5b90e20f388d925ce8c8]] The keycap set is great in terms of design, modifier key size options, and key legend for quality for price. Fitting for all keys is great, excepts for the large spacebars.
The space bars (both 6.25u and 7u) in my set don’t work smoothly and somehow get the stabs stuck (two different kinds of stabs as well). I tried them on two keyboards (one with 7u and other with 6.25u) and only these spacebars showed problems and the other spacebars I have do not show this problem. I am not sure really whats causing the space bar issue, maybe those pbt space keycaps are slightly bent (as I have read about) but visually they look okay.

reviews.title: Good Quality for the $ - Fits Cherry MX Switches
reviews.text:
This fit my Ducky mechanical keyboard without issue. They seem to be well made and fit tightly in my cherry MX switches. A couple observations, not sure it’s a positive or a negative but a point of observation:

1) They do not come with media key replacements. The blue ones you see in my image are from my original key caps

2) The height of the keys are slightly shorter than my Ducky OEMs. While initially this was a possible concern for me, having typed with the new keys for a couple weeks, seems to not impact typing at all.

3) The blue caps that came with the replacements are not the ones you see in my photo (Enter key). They have a slightly yellow / blue hue to them vs. the Ducky’s I’m using. Wanted my enter key to match the rest of the blue media keys so I mixed and matched. Some may like the blue that comes with the set, I just preferred my original blue keys color.

For the price, they fit the bill, can’t complain. We’ll see long term how the printing of the keys holds up / how quickly they wear down.

Wish list:

- Caps lock key had the word ‘Caps’ on it
- Windows button keys had something besides “Super”.

Overall good purchase.

title: 4-Port HDMI KVM Switch – 4K 60Hz – QHD 144Hz - Audio Output and USB Sharing – Mechanical & Gaming Keyboards Supported – HKS0401A2U
description:
This 4x1 HDMI KVM Switch allows you to share one set of “keyboard, video monitor, and mouse” (“KVM”) with up to four PC’s or video devices (Xbox, PS4, Firestick, etc). The switcher does not require software and works with Windows, MacOS, Unix/Linux, Raspberri Pi, etc. HDMI 2.0 protocol supports 4K@60hz and blazing 144hz@1440P, making this device a perfect fit for work-from-home professionals and high-end video gaming.
Key Features
- Supports DisplayPort, DVI, & VGA with approved adapters.  - Keyboard passthrough mode allows for better compatibility plus multimedia key functions  - Hotkey triggers assignable to either Scroll Lock or Right-CTRL  - USB sharing works with printers, jump drives, USB headsets and webcams for teleconferencing.  - Auto-scanning with adjustable time interval from 1 to 250 seconds.  - 1-year US-based warranty when purchased from BuyTESmart
FAQ
- Audio is transmitted via HDMI connection. HDMI or DisplayPort monitor required for audio  - Use powered USB hub to expand the USB2.0 port if multiple connections are needed  - Use USB extender to reduce distance to receiver if wireless mouse/keyboard is “jumpy”  - Please install CR2025 battery before use (battery not included)
Mouse / Keyboard Compatibility
Dedicated mouse & keyboard ports have emulation programming. Second gen chipsets have “Passthrough Mode” to send special characters such as multimedia keys (play, pause, volume up/down), which improves keyboard compatibility. Keyboard software such as Logitech Options, Corsair iCUE, etc conflicts with the emulation and limits the ability to use macro keys. We recommend using the KVM’s USB2.0 port as a workaround if this software if needed. USB2.0 port does not have emulation programming and KVM will not recognize hotkey commands. Please use a powered USB hub to expand USB connectivity.
features: Share 4 computers with 1 monitor, keyboard, mouse. Switch between PC’s with push button, keyboard hotkeys, mouse gestures, or remote control. Can also use as HDMI switch for gaming consoles and streaming devices.
HDMI 2.0 supports UHD 4K60hz (3840x2160), QHD 144hz (2560x1440), 1080P 144hz (1920x1080). Plus Ultrawide 60hz (21:9 & 32:9). HDCP 2.2, HDR10, Dolby Vision. EDID Emulation.
Plug & Play. No software or drivers needed. Supported by IT departments for work-from-home professionals. Separate your work from your personal life without the hassle & clutter!
Transmit audio to monitor via HDMI and/or external speakers via stereo output. Share USB device such as webcam and headset with dedicated USB2.0 port. Great for Zoom and Twitch users.
Supports wired, wireless, mechanical, multimedia, and gaming keyboards. No delay, zero latency keyboard switching and pass-through mode for multimedia keys. READ FAQ & PICS.
U.S. Based TECHNICAL SUPPORT from BuyTESmart available to help resolve all issues.

reviews.title: Mostly good
reviews.text: One of my connected PCs has a hard time getting the resolution to 1440, cannot figure out why.  I have to restart to get it to go from 1080 to 1440.

reviews.title: Great KVM switch
reviews.text: I wasn’t sure what to expect , but after having it for a week I really like the switch.  Perfect size and functionality.

Support PCB Mounted and Plate Mounted stabilizers, we recommend you to use PCB Mounted stabilizers. 32U4 chip master, support QMK/VIA, more customized, you can use it to modify key functions and customize more functions. Wired mode, supports USB-C, typing response is faster.

【Alice Layout】The Alice layout considers the natural input angle of the wrist from the perspective of ergonomics. When using the conventional arrangement keyboard for continuous high-intensity input work, the unnatural bending of the wrist will easily lead to hand fatigue, and the Alice layout can solve this problem very well, it takes into account the integrity of the keyboard and the relatively relaxed input state of the wrist.

【South-facing RGB Backlit】Alice66 has 66 lamp beads + 16 bottom lamps, 16 million color RGB backlight, the RBG Backlight effect is very bright even in the daytime. The lower lamp position is designed so that the light will not be blocked by the switch. The fully transparent bottom cover allows the 16 bottom lights to display their backlight effects without obstruction, making the keyboard look more attractive.

【Acrylic Gasket Structure】Alice66 is designed with Gasket structure, equipped with Pom material positioning plate, poron sandwich surface, and poron pad, the sound is pure, and the feel is soft. This wonderful feel can make the typing process very pleasant and full of fun.

reviews.title: Sounds great with no mods
reviews.text:
[[VIDEOID:58cc2520e806ff1428542a1df7456920]] Assembly was pretty easy for this DIY kit. It even sounds great without any modifications. I decided to put poron switch pads on the pcb since the foam it came with did not line up properly. The feet that comes with this kit is too aggressive for my liking. I decided to remove it and put clear rubber bumpons

Did not come with stabilizers. I was hoping it does at this price point. Speaking of which, this kit can be had for cheaper on other retailers. But ordering from Amazon gave me more confidence.

My copy came with cracked and scuffed case. I messaged the seller directly and they were able to help and send me a new case promptly. They also helped with VIA setup on this keyboard.

Pros:
- easy assembly
- VIA support PCB
- great sound with little to no modifications
- south facing, per key RGB
- under glow lighting
- A+ great customer support from seller

Cons:
- price seems higher compared to other websites
- inconsistent craftsmanship
- no stabilizers included
- front of the keyboard can be wobbly with the aggressive feet riser installed

(Keycaps: MT3 2048; Switches: WS Aurora Clears)

reviews.title: Just what I was looking for
reviews.text: Great quality, love that it comes with multiple different colors of the same key so that you can customize it. So happy with this purchase!!!

reviews.title: Good feeling, good overall design
reviews.text: Was able to use these keys on my gaming keyboard.  It provided a good feeling as I press on the keys.  It’s Very responsive.  The only downside to this is that the rgb lights on my keyboard does not pass through the keys…not the end of the world but overall a good set of keys.  The picture on the website is deceiving as it’s just keys to replace an existing keyboard.  It does not come with the keyboard itself.

reviews.title: You Like Red, White & Blue So Make Your Keyboard Reflect That!
reviews.text: We started doing this with our “high-end” computer buyers. We take a premium – Cherry or similar – keyboard and remove all or most of the key tops replacing them with the various colors available making a color complementary computer input device. It makes for a more interesting keyboard, which obviously is the main interface with your computer, and it gives our customers something so unique as to be one-of-a-kind. All the keyboards start with premium red, blue, brown, or black key actuators which, without exception, are the best available. That together with our customer’s keytops “artwork” gives a person something exclusive as well as offering superior functionality. The keytops come right off use the small plastic tool most of the keytop provides include with their kits and doing a whole keyboard only takes about 20 minutes. One thing you do have to watch for is the keytop height. Many times, they will make certain keys a little higher. This poses no issue unless you stick a high key in the middle of the number/letters, then this may impede faster typists. Remember each key actuator that is attached to the base of the keyboard is programmed to send a certain signal to the computer thus causing your monitor to display that letter/number or take that action. For example, if you put the “M” keytop where the “T” was that will still send a “T” to your computer and monitor. I would recommend removing and replacing 2 or 3 at a time or having a like keyboard to refer to, so the possibility for confusion is limited. All keyboards don’t have the exact layout especially in the extended section and number pad. Also, some of the higher end gaming style keyboards have extra programmable keys that are specified through the keyboard manufacturer’s software. Some of the keytop set will provide blank keys to use in these instances. Buy a couple sets and have fun making your own custom keyboard.

reviews.title: Great color for white keyboard, good quality
reviews.text: The NACODEX 129 Keys PBT Keycaps Set is a high-quality and visually appealing upgrade for any mechanical keyboard. The keycaps are made of durable PBT material and feature a JSA profile and double shot design. The pink and white color combination gives a unique touch to the keyboard, making it a great choice for those who want to personalize their setup. The keycaps are compatible with Cherry, Gateron, Otemu, and Kailh switches, making them a versatile choice for many different types of mechanical keyboards. Overall, this keycap set is a great investment for anyone looking to improve the look and feel of their keyboard.

reviews.title: Very nice coiled keyboard cable.
reviews.text:
The cable came nicely coiled with no flaws. Plugs are high quality and the screw connection type is pretty heavy duty I don't see this breaking. Only wished the straight part of the cable was longer.

I highly recommend this cable for anyone looking for a custom looking cable that wont break the bank.

reviews.title: Works as advertised!
reviews.text: This cable worked exactly as advertised. My keyboard has a USB c connector, and it fit together perfectly. I love the look of this cable as it neatly tucks under my desk and doesn't leave any unnecessary excess hanging around. Overall very happy with this product!

reviews.title: Decent aviator cable for my keyboard setup.
reviews.text: These cables are an aesthetically great match for my “rebel scum” keyboard build.  The connections are tight and the materials used seems like they are great quality.  I don’t really have any complaints about the quality of this product.  I think it’s important to note these are not braided cables, and have a soft rubber feel, much like you would get on any standard USB type cable.

reviews.title: Good Quality!
reviews.text: Bright white coiled cable makes my desk look neater and organized. Matches my desk decor better. Keyboard response is the same as the original cable it came with, no complaints here.

reviews.title: Treat your keyboard to a nicer cable
reviews.text: What a nice cable, with solid metal USB plugs, a clean aviator connector, and tight and even coils. I got it in purple, to match some keycaps I like, and it looks perfect. The USB-C plug is about 5.5mm thick, so should fit most recessed plugs. I was able to charge every device I tried, even a chromebook (lenovo duet) and phone (pixel 6a).

title: 66 Pieces Gateron Yellow Switches for Mechanical Keyboards,3-pin KS-9 Supporting SMD RGB Light | Linear | 50g Force
description:
Specifications：
Gateron Red Switch（Office）---Linear---Bottom out distances4±0.4mm---Actuation distances2.0±0.6mm---Actuation force45±15gf---Bottom out force60±15gfGateron Brown Switch（Universal axis）---Tactilre&Clicky---Bottom out distances4±0.4mm---Actuation distances2.0±0.6mm---Actuation force55±15gf---Bottom out force70±15gfGateron Blue Switch（Typing artifact）---Tactilre&Clicky---Bottom out distances4±0.4mm---Actuation distances2.3±0.6mm---Actuation force60±15gf---Bottom out force70±15gfGateron Yellow Switch（Smooth）---Linear---Bottom out distances4±0.4mm---Actuation distances2.0±0.6mm---Actuation force50±15gf---Bottom out force65±15gfGateron Black Switch（Game）---Linear---Bottom out distances4±0.4mm---Actuation distances2.0±0.6mm---Actuation force60±15gf---Bottom out force75±15gfGateron White Switch（Light）---Linear---Bottom out distances4±0.4mm---Actuation distances2.0±0.6mm---Actuation force35±15gf---Bottom out force50±15gfGateron Green Switch（Potent）---Tactilre&Clicky---Bottom out distances4±0.4mm---Actuation distances2.3±0.6mm---Actuation force70±15gf---Bottom out force80±15gf
Note：
During the delivery process, the pins of the switch may touch and bend. This is inevitable. Bent pins can be straightened with nail clippers, which will not affect normal use.
features: Switches Compatibility：Gateron KS-9 RGB Mechanical MX Type Key Switch-Clear top，Supports two-pin 3mm round lights, four-pin RGB lights and patch RGB lights.Compatible with Cherry MX switch, suitable for most mechanical keyboards.
Main Parameters：Gateron Yellow Switch---Linear feel (straight up and down), Actuation force is about 50±15gf.Over 50 million laboratory tests ensure durability and more stable and reliable quality.

Suitable For：Gateron Yellow Switch---The yellow switch feels smooth, rebounds faster, and feels stronger when pressed. You can feel it making the keycap follow your fingers more closely. The yellow switch is our recommended switch for those who feel that the red switch is too light, too soft, and needs a little stronger feedback.
Installation Type:Plate mounted switches (3 pins)
Package Included：1 puller tool and 66 Yellow Switches ‘+’ shape and PP protection box.

reviews.title: Almost perfect :)
reviews.text: A few had slightly bent pins,  but safely fixed with tweezers. Crisp clicking on the Blues. Would recommend if asked.

title: Wired Keyboard and Mouse Combo, FEDARFOX Full Size Backlit Illuminated Rainbow Keyboard and Mouse Set Gaming Keyboard for Windows, Computer, Desktop, PC,PS (Black)
description: Keyboard and Mouse Combo, Compact Full Size Rainbow Keyboard and Mouse Set Backlit Illuminated Mice Keyboard for Windows, Computer, Desktop, PC, Notebook
features: Keyboard and Mouse Combo - The wired keyboard is made of aluminum alloy brushed panel, which is sturdy and durable. Plug & Play,the cord of the keyboard and mosue is 1.2m and long enought to connect your PC port.The gaming keyboard features a streamlined layout that is ergonomically designed for gaming or office or home use

Stylish Keyboard and Practical Design: The newest rainbow keyboard is not like the ordinary keyboard, and made bold innovations such as the removable Hand-Rest (no need screw) ,phone holder and floating keycap are perfectly integrated nto the design, making the keyboard not only have the beauty of technology, but also more practical and worth

Backlit Gaming Mouse - The wired mouse has Intelligent colorful rainbow breathing lights, design back and go ahead functions of left side button , 4 adjustable Dpi levels(800-1200-2400-3200) and non-slip design, provide accurate positioning and responsive, the gaming experience is more vivid
Wide Compatibility: It compatible with windows10/8/7, windows vista, windows Xp, mac,Android. Support for PC desktop laptop computer Google TV smart TV, and works well for Ps4 Game / Xbox One

Easy to Use & Ergonomic Keyboard Mouse - Just plug to your device's port and play , easy to use. Ergonomic design of both wired keyboard and mouse release your hand from fatigue, and the professional mechanical feeling bring you fantastic usage experience

reviews.title: Solid
reviews.text: This keyboard and mouse set is solid.  They have a durable aluminum body.  The keys are mechanical in look, but press easy without any noise.  The colors are nice and shine through the keys so you can see which letter it is in the dark.  It gives off just the right amount of light.  They look slick together on a desk and operate well.  Great little set.

reviews.title: Solid
reviews.text: This keyboard and mouse set is solid.  They have a durable aluminum body.  The keys are mechanical in look, but press easy without any noise.  The colors are nice and shine through the keys so you can see which letter it is in the dark.  It gives off just the right amount of light.  They look slick together on a desk and operate well.  Great little set.

reviews.title: Nice keyboard!!
reviews.text:
Updated review.. The mouse quit working after about 12 hours of use. This is disappointing because when it worked it was nice and responsive and was comfortable. When I left the original review everything worked fine..

This keyboard looks and works good. The back lighting makes it easy to see in the dark and the keys are precise and feel good while typing. The real aluminum frame is sturdy and looks good too. The phone holder is a nice touch. I would say it's worth it for the price.

title: Akko Keycaps Top Printed Shine-Through with ASA Profile, White Keycaps PBT Double-Shot Keycaps for Mechanical Keyboards
features: SHINE-THROUGH KEYCAPS – Deliver premium and shining RGB experiences with Akko ASA Shine-through keycap sets! Come with extremely durable and resistant double-shot Shine-through keycaps, the legend shine through keycaps brings unique and great RGB effects.

ULTIMATE CUSTOMIZATION FLEXIBILITY – Comes with Akko ASA profile, the keycap set is suitable for a variety of layouts including 61-key(60%), 64-key, 68-key (65%), 84-key (75%), 87-key (TKL), 98-Key (Classic 1800), 100-Key (96%), Alice and full 108-key size, and thus offers strong flexibility for keyboard personalization.

ASA PROFILE – Akko’s proprietary ASA profile is tailored to offer a more familiar and comfortable typing experience by matching the height for OEM profile while maintaining the aesthetics and spheric top of the classic SA profile.
PBT DOUBLE-SHOT – Double-shot keycaps are extremely durable and resistant to wear even for decades, and the legends are particularly vibrant, sharp with high contrast.

FOR MX STRUCTURE SWITCHES – Compatible with MX structure mechanical switches and mainstream keyboards including but not limited to Cherry, Gateron, Akko and Kailh. Large keys of non-standard layout & stabilizers may not be compatible.

reviews.title: Not the brightest shine-through, but for the material and price it's worth it.
reviews.text:
Akko's ASA profile is a taller profile kind of like SA. Feels great to type on and is fine for gaming if you don't mind the extra travel time on each keystroke. The finish on these has a great texture and it's PBT so it shouldn't wear out quickly. "Shouldn't" being relative since I tend to shred keycaps as though my fingers were first dipped in acid before typing.

30.00 for keycaps of this quality and for the number of keys is great especially in a hobby where everything is over priced, over hyped, and overly bogged down in group buys and pre-orders that take literal years to fulfill.

reviews.title: Shine-Through keycaps, look amazing
reviews.text: These shine-through keycaps look awesome.  They are super easy to install, like most keycaps.  It is just a bit time consuming.  I love being able to change out keys and switches and seem to collect keycaps.  These are great and sound and feel amazing.

title: Code V3 104-Key Illuminated Mechanical Keyboard - White LED Backlighting, Black Case (Cherry MX Brown)
features: White LED backlighting w/7 levels of brightness. Onboard memory saves your lighting preference
Fully programmable via hardware: program macros and keymaps over four layers
No obnoxious branding with programmable RGB indicator lights
Slim case design w/multi-channel cable routing system and detachable USB cable

reviews.title: Buy it
reviews.text:
If you use a keyboard 8 hours a day, make it count. Use a keyboard that you enjoy.
The Code keyboard is one of my favorites. The clear switches make it so you have a great feel - but don’t annoy your coworkers.

title: dagaladoo XVX 189 Keys Double Shot Keycaps, PBT Custom Keyboard Keycaps Full Set, XVX Profile Keycaps for 60% 65% 70% 100% Cherry Gateron MX Switches Mechanical Keyboard, Black/Purple
features: Full 189 keys PBT keycaps set, including 108 standard keys, 61 supplementary keys and 20 invert color supplementary keys for most 61/64/66/68/84/87/96/98/100/104/108 mechanical keyboards.
Double shot design keycaps, made from two colors material molded into each others, make the keycaps characters maintain the vibrance and saturation, clear and not fade.
XVX profile keycaps, ergonomic height design similar than Cherry profile, while increasing the contact surface between the finger and keycaps, bring you a comfortable typing experience.
Made from high content PBT material, 0.2cm extra-thick keycaps wall, which is provide top-notch durability, excellent touch and grease resistance.
Minimalist theme style and classic color matching, with a large number of personalized complement keys, you can DIY different styles of keyboards according to your own ideas.

reviews.title: Fits Royal Kludge 90% and Keychron K10!
reviews.text: I like all the extras. The quality of the PBT is grainy in texture, so if that's the kind of thing you're in to, then these won't disappoint. Not shine through, although the advertisement never claims they are. No light is coming through these from your board. The XVX profile is nice. Many extra key options- this set has fit several boards from several brands- not something you see for the price point! Quality printing and nice font on the caps. The keys feel good to type on when used with a clicky or linear switch. They are a bit heavy, so a lighter switch wouldn't be a bad option. I don't like to use these on tactile switches due to the mushy that these caps add to the keys. O Rings did not help these keys IMO.

reviews.title: Random assortment of loose keys
reviews.text: Expected a full set of keycaps, instead received a box containing three bags of loose keys, missing multiple letters, numbers, function keys, etc. Might be good for a concept art project, but not for a working keyboard.

reviews.title: Some keys misaligned with switch stems.
reviews.text: The keys have a nice touch, sound and printing however some keys, particularly my "O" key are a misaligned with the switch stem causing it to stick up a little higher than the "I" and "P" on either side. I did not mistake a "0" for an "O".

reviews.title: Seem to be good quality, but I hate the XVX profile
reviews.text:
These seems to be good quality keycaps, I have no issues with them at all. I have put them on multiple boards now, and I haven't had any break or scratch. Colors are accurate and legends are clear. No complaints there.

My one gripe is the XVX profile. It takes a lot of getting used to (like any new profile would), but the surface area of the keys is much larger than most other profiles. Therefore, when typing, it feels as if your fingers have to travel significantly further to reach the next key. It also makes it much, much easier to fat finger extra keys. If I had realized how much more surface area there was, I would not have purchased these. However, they do look great, they were cheap, and they sound pretty good too.

reviews.title: Great keycaps, andd even better customer service!
reviews.text: I wanted to find some PBT keycaps that were double-shot and gave some aesthetic flair to my $15 108 full sized mech keyboard with brown switches.  They fit just fine, and give a different, yet, pleasing sounds and feel to the keyboard.  I wanted non-see through keycaps in order to hide most of the LED rainbow glare.  The backlit effect is, again, very pleasing.  The keycap set that I originally ordered was missing the F9 key.  I contacted their customer service through Amazon, and got a reply back right away and arranged for a replacement set to be shipped out.  Got it the new set the day after, and it was complete.  Will purchase from this seller again whenever I need/want new keycaps.  Very confident if something is wrong, will be taken care of in a timely matter.
